summary: Matched the Link bow item object by replacing the 648-line itlinkbow assembly stub with a 267-line C implementation, moving itlinkbow.c from NonMatching/asm to Matching/src in the build, and correcting related item/fighter function prototypes. The PR also marked several itlinkbow internal callbacks as local in symbols.txt after the author identified how to distinguish global versus local functions in objdiff. The implementation models Link/Young Link/Kirby bow state transitions, scale preservation, owner tracking, and item animation switching, while adding itLinkBowVars to the item union for xDD4 item-specific storage.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/it/items/itlinkbow.s and implemented the object in src/melee/it/items/itlinkbow.c.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itlinkbow.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itlinkbow.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build src/melee/it/items/itlinkbow.c.o instead of asm/melee/it/items/itlinkbow.s.o.

Added C implementations and declarations for it_802AF1A4, it_802AF298, it_802AF304, it_802AF434, it_802AF920, and static callbacks it_802AF32C, it_802AF64C, it_802AF844, it_802AF848, it_802AF90C, it_802AF914, and it_802AF918.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F6E98 and static state mapping arrays it_803F6F08 and it_803F6F24 in itlinkbow.c.
Added itLinkBowVars with fields f32 x0 and HSD_GObj* x4 in itCharItems.h, and added linkbow to Item::xDD4_itemVar in src/melee/it/types.h.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark it_802AF32C, it_802AF64C, it_802AF844, it_802AF848, it_802AF90C, it_802AF914, and it_802AF918 as scope:local instead of scope:global.
Corrected several owner/attachment part parameters from raw u8 to Fighter_Part, including Item_8026AB54, it_8026B9A8, it_8029A748, it_802C6F40, it_802ADC54, and it_802BDA64.
Corrected Kirby Link-special helper prototypes in ftKb_Init.h: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394 returns ftLk_SpecialNIndex and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB470 returns bool.
Moved itlinkbow declarations out of broad it_27CF.h and into src/melee/it/items/itlinkbow.h, then included itlinkbow.h where needed in ftLk_SpecialN.c.
smart_moves: Used objdiff symbol scope information to demote internal itlinkbow callbacks from global to static/local, matching the object file more accurately and reducing exported API noise.

Introduced item-specific xDD4 storage as itLinkBowVars instead of leaving magic offsets 0xDD4 and 0xDD8 implicit; x0 stores the owner-derived scale from ftLib_800869D4 and x4 stores the original owner GObj.
Preserved the Link bow's animation frame when switching from one mapped animation state to another via Item_80268DD4 after Item_80268E5C and Item_802694CC.

Modeled both Link/CLink and Kirby copied bow flows in the same switch logic, using ftLk_SpecialN_GetIndex for Link kinds and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394/ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB444/ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB470 for Kirby bow kinds.

Replaced raw u8 part parameters with Fighter_Part across related item spawn/attach functions, making function signatures reflect actual semantics and helping future matches avoid casts or incorrect promotions.
Kept small do-nothing and terminal callbacks as local static functions, mirroring item state table usage instead of unnecessarily exposing them through headers.
decomp_lessons: For item objects with many state callbacks, verify objdiff local/global status before declaring prototypes; static callbacks should also be marked scope:local in symbols.txt.
When an item stores owner-derived state in xDD4, add a named item-var struct and union member rather than repeatedly accessing raw offsets.

Function signatures should propagate semantic enum types like Fighter_Part and ftLk_SpecialNIndex through all callers and headers; otherwise matching work can be blocked by inconsistent parameter widths or return types.
Bow items need careful owner validation: the code checks that the current owner is the originally stored owner before syncing animation state or deciding destruction.
If an item changes animation state while logically continuing the same action, save x5CC_currentAnimFrame and restore it with Item_80268DD4 after the state change.
Broad item headers such as it_27CF.h should not accumulate unrelated item-specific prototypes when a dedicated item header exists.
The PR body explicitly says the author still did not know how to properly match data components, so confidence about intentional data matching choices beyond the diff is limited.
naming_conventions: Item-specific variable structs use the item name plus Vars, here itLinkBowVars.
The item union member is lowercase item-specific naming, here item->xDD4_itemVar.linkbow.
Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as it_802AF1A4 and it_802AF298.
Internal callbacks are declared static in the C file and marked scope:local in symbols.txt.
Fighter-specific helper names distinguish Link and Kirby copy behavior: ftLk_SpecialN_* versus ftKb_SpecialNLk*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the entire itlinkbow assembly file once the C implementation matched.
Switched build inputs from asm/melee/it/items/itlinkbow.s.o to src/melee/it/items/itlinkbow.c.o.
Changed configure.py classification for itlinkbow.c to Matching.
Used static function declarations to align with local symbol scope for item state callbacks.
Added a padding local f32 pad[1] in it_802AF1A4, likely to shape stack layout; the exact rationale is not discussed in PR text.
Used chained assignment scale.x = scale.y = scale.z = item->xDD4_itemVar.linkbow.x0 to match repeated scale writes before HSD_JObjSetScale.
Used state lookup arrays it_803F6F08 and it_803F6F24 to reproduce animation-state mapping from assembly.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Author note: they figured out how to tell which functions are global versus local in objdiff.
Author note: they apologized that they still did not know how to properly match data components of the object file.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename it_802AF1A4 or other itlinkbow functions to semantic names.
Find PRs that improve or match the data components around it_803F6E98, it_803F6F08, or it_803F6F24.
Search for further cleanup of Fighter_Part prototype propagation in item spawn functions.
Search for later struct-field naming of itLinkBowVars.x0 and itLinkBowVars.x4.
Check whether any review or follow-up PR addressed the author's uncertainty about matching data components.
